timeline.label: Q1 2026: revenue +18% to $2.7B; Nights and Seats Booked 156M (+9%); GBV $29.2B; Adjusted EBITDA $519M (19% margin); FCF $1.7B
FY2025 10-K: revenue $12.2B (+10%); nights 533M (+8%); GBV $91.3B (+12%); FCF $4.6B; net income $2.5B; Adjusted EBITDA $4.3B
Repurchased 29.7M Class A shares for $3.8B in 2025; $5.6B remaining under August 2025 authorization
Board approved new $6.0B share repurchase program (August 2025)
February 2024 share repurchase program authorized; completed in 2025
Expanded brand platform beyond core stays into experiences and services; KPI renamed Nights and Seats Booked
Post-pandemic travel recovery drove GBV and nights re-acceleration after 2020–2021 disruption
IPO on Nasdaq (ABNB); dual-class structure with founder super-voting shares
COVID-19 shock: global nights collapsed; company cut costs and later benefited from domestic and long-term-stay demand
International expansion scaled; multi-region GBV mix reduced single-city concentration risk
Founded in San Francisco as marketplace connecting hosts and guests for short-term stays

industry.structure: Global short-term rental is oligopolistic at the platform layer (Airbnb, Booking, Vrbo/Expedia) with fragmented supply (millions of hosts). Hotels and metasearch remain parallel distribution. Regulatory lodging taxes and local short-term rental bans are the main structural frictions.
industry.share_shift: Airbnb is gaining nights and gross booking value (+8% nights, +12% GBV in FY2025) with strength across all regions; experiences and services remain a small mix versus stays but management is investing for attach.
industry.trend: AI-assisted search and trip planning, long-term stays (28+ nights), and cross-border travel normalization are current growth drivers; cities continue tightening STR rules and lodging-tax collection, which raises compliance cost but also barriers for small entrants.
